prompt:
You are the Product Manager agent critiquing a drafted Method artifact, following Juval Lowy's The Method.
Respond with a single JSON object. No prose outside the JSON.
Artifact under review: ScrubbedRequirements
Your JSON output MUST conform exactly to this JSON Schema (notes is required when verdict=2/Revise: the revision the architect should make).
Schema: {"properties":{"verdict":{"type":"integer","enum":[1,2]},"notes":{"type":"string"}},"additionalProperties":false,"type":"object","required":["verdict","notes"]}

Draft under review:
{"items":[{"id":"SR-1","statement":"The system shall govern the lifecycle of an event from initial draft through its active life to conclusion."},{"id":"SR-2","statement":"The system shall maintain the relationships between an event and the members associated with it."},{"id":"SR-3","statement":"The system shall enable organizers to create and govern events and engage others to gather."},{"id":"SR-4","statement":"The system shall capture a member's intent to attend an event."},{"id":"SR-5","statement":"The system shall coordinate RSVP, comment, and reminder so that intent to attend is captured and nurtured into participation."},{"id":"SR-6","statement":"The system shall ensure that a member's expressed intent to attend reliably translates into participation."},{"id":"SR-7","statement":"The system shall reach the community about events and engagement regardless of which notification channel is used."},{"id":"SR-8","statement":"The system shall absorb the addition or change of notification channels without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-9","statement":"The system shall recognize members and organizers regardless of the chosen identity provider."},{"id":"SR-10","statement":"The system shall keep member and organizer relationships constant even as the underlying identity provider changes."},{"id":"SR-11","statement":"The system shall absorb the addition or change of identity providers without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-12","statement":"The system shall mediate between an event and external information about where it is held."},{"id":"SR-13","statement":"The system shall allow the place of an event to be sourced or changed without altering the event's structure."},{"id":"SR-14","statement":"The system shall absorb the addition or change of venue data sources without disrupting the organizing experience."},{"id":"SR-15","statement":"The system shall onboard a new notification channel, identity provider, or venue data source within one release cycle and at no more than 15% incremental engineering cost."},{"id":"SR-16","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring facts about an event and its relationships to members."},{"id":"SR-17","statement":"The system shall retain the state of members' RSVPs, comments, and reminders for events."},{"id":"SR-18","statement":"The system shall retain the enduring association between members or organizers and their recognized identity."},{"id":"SR-19","statement":"The system shall retain the association between an event and its resolved place."}]}

Task: as the Product Manager, ratify the draft (Approve) or request a concrete revision (Revise with notes). Ratify only what faithfully serves the business; the human makes the final commit decision.

response.notes:
The draft is largely sound in structure and decomposition but requires the following concrete revisions before ratification:

1. SR-5 and SR-6 are redundant with SR-4. SR-4 captures intent; SR-5 describes the mechanics (RSVP, comment, reminder) that nurture intent; SR-6 claims intent 'reliably translates into participation.' SR-6 introduces an unverifiable guarantee ('reliably translates') that conflates system responsibility with human behavior. Either delete SR-6 or restate it as: 'The system shall track the progression of a member's RSVP from expressed intent through confirmed attendance.' This keeps the requirement within the system boundary.

2. SR-3 overlaps significantly with SR-1 and SR-2. SR-1 governs the event lifecycle; SR-2 governs member relationships. SR-3 restates both from the organizer's perspective without adding a distinct system responsibility. If an organizer-specific decomposition is needed, it should be a decomposition of SR-1/SR-2, not a peer item. Either remove SR-3 or clarify what it covers that SR-1 and SR-2 do not (e.g., access-control rules specific to the organizer role).

3. SR-15 mixes two distinct concerns: a time-box constraint (one release cycle) and a cost constraint (15% incremental engineering cost). These are separate non-functional requirements and should be expressed as separate items so each can be independently verified and tracked. Split into SR-15a (onboarding timeline) and SR-15b (onboarding cost ceiling).

4. SR-16 ('enduring facts about an event') and SR-19 ('association between an event and its resolved place') are too vague for ratification. 'Enduring facts' is undefined. Specify what facts are enduring (e.g., title, scheduled time, organizer identity, attendance record) so engineers and QA have a testable target. SR-19 should clarify whether 'resolved place' means the final confirmed venue or any historical snapshot of venue data.

5. No requirement addresses data privacy or member consent (e.g., retention limits, right-to-erasure expectations). Given that the system retains member identity associations (SR-18) and RSVP history (SR-17), at least one scrubbed requirement should bound the system's obligation to honor member data-removal requests. Add: 'The system shall support removal of a member's personal associations upon request without corrupting the structural integrity of the events they attended.'

Please revise and resubmit.
